rows.title: P0: Re-audit 36 SKILL references — inline execution-discipline protocols back to body
rows.summary: v2.26.0 progressive-loading extracted 36 protocols; Codex dogfood proved Blake skips Layer 2 / completion report because the load_when triggers are circular (agent doesn't know step exists without reading the reference). Re-audit each file, move execution-discipline content back to SKILL body, verify on both platforms.
rows.cost_rationale: 36 reference files must each be individually classified (circular vs non-circular trigger), then SKILL.md surgically expanded with SAFETY-count verification throughout.
rows.value_rationale: Directly restores broken execution quality: Blake skipping Layer 2 and completion reports is a P0 protocol compliance failure observed in live Codex dogfood.
rows.deliverable: Updated alex/blake SKILL.md with >=200 lines reinstated from references/ + both .claude/skills and .agents/skills platform verification passing (diff shows only sanctioned reference-ok protocols remain extracted).

rows.title: Gate ROI Measurement — Prove or Falsify TAD's Core Quality Claim
rows.summary: The 2026-06-09 repositioning stress-test (O1/KR2) named 'gate-ROI unproven' as an explicit new gap. TAD's positioning as a quality framework depends on Gates catching real defects, but no measurement exists. Analyze ≥20 historical handoffs from .tad/evidence/traces/ to classify Gate-caught defects by severity and counterfactual impact, producing a verdict: gates net-positive / net-neutral / net-negative vs a no-gate baseline.
rows.cost_rationale: Read-only trace analysis across 56 JSONL trace files; no code changes; single output report.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR3 explicitly lists 'gate-ROI unproven' as a new gap; also advances O2/KR1 by producing evidence-grounded upgrade direction (mechanical vs soft enforcement). Without this measurement TAD's core quality positioning remains rhetorical per the repositioning-3-walls research verdict.
rows.deliverable: /Users/sheldonzhao/01-on progress programs/TAD/.tad/evidence/research/gate-roi-measurement-2026-07.md — analysis of ≥20 handoffs, defect classification table, counterfactual verdict, and a go/no-go recommendation on investing in mechanical gate enforcement

rows.title: NotebookLM Deep-Ask Rounds 4+5 — Staleness Trap and Human Skill Growth Evidence
rows.summary: O3/KR3 requires ≥5 cross-source synthesis rounds saved; only 3 exist. The 2026-06-09 research named two unanswered questions: (1) 'Staleness Trap' — how does CLAUDE.md/project-knowledge stay current as Claude capabilities evolve? (2) 'Human skill growth' — is there evidence users gain permanent independent skill or only AI-augmented output? These are the exact research questions for rounds 4 and 5 using the active TAD Evolution Research notebook (37cfefa5, 45 sources).
rows.cost_rationale: Two NotebookLM query sessions plus write-up; no code changes; builds on existing 45-source notebook.
rows.value_rationale: Directly closes O3/KR3 (stalled at 3/5 rounds since May) and simultaneously advances O1/KR3 (the two named gaps each map to an O1 capability gap requiring severity assessment).
rows.deliverable: Two research findings files: /Users/sheldonzhao/01-on progress programs/TAD/.tad/evidence/research/2026-07-staleness-trap-findings.md and /Users/sheldonzhao/01-on progress programs/TAD/.tad/evidence/research/2026-07-human-skill-growth-findings.md — each with ≥3 cross-source synthesis points and citations. Together these bring O3/KR3 from 3 to 5 rounds (status: DONE).

rows.title: Pack Behavioral Examples Scaffold — html-anything examples/ Pattern for 3 Pilot Packs
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527-pack-behavioral-examples was promoted but is absent from the backlog. Both Codex and Gemini independent audits named 'validation theater' (13/13 installed confirms file operations, not quality) as a P0 — now codified in principles.md YOLO Audit Findings. The html-anything example.html pattern (each skill ships with a ground-truth output fixture) is the identified solution. Implement examples/ directories for 3 pilot packs with a pack-eval.sh verifier and update Gate 3 to require examples/ for new packs.
rows.cost_rationale: Multiple new files across 3 packs plus a new verifier script and a gate/SKILL.md modification that touches SAFETY zones; L-class scope.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1 explicitly lists behavioral eval as an upgrade direction (rank #3 in 2026-05-14 prioritization matrix); closes the 'validation theater' P0 from cross-model audit (YOLO Audit Findings principle); the pattern is proven from html-anything evidence.
rows.deliverable: examples/ directory with 2 input/output fixture pairs in each of 3 packs (ai-agent-architecture, web-frontend, code-security); /Users/sheldonzhao/01-on progress programs/TAD/.tad/hooks/lib/pack-eval.sh running fixtures and checking expected output markers; Gate 3 checklist item 'examples/ present and passing pack-eval.sh' added to gate/SKILL.md

rows.title: Fix tad.sh detect_state glob arms before next version bump
rows.summary: tad.sh arms 2.1*/2.2* (~lines 305-313) will misclassify 3-part versions like 2.19.x as v2.0 once TARGET_VERSION moves past 2.19.1. Must be fixed before the next release.
rows.cost_rationale: Surgical single-file fix with a clear test case; no new abstraction.
rows.value_rationale: Known time-bomb that will corrupt version detection at the next release; fix now costs minutes vs debugging a silent misclassification in production.
rows.deliverable: Updated tad.sh detect_state arms using precise 3-part version pattern matching + regression fixture confirming 2.20.0 and 2.33.0 are not misclassified as v2.0.

rows.title: TAD Repositioning — Capability Acquisition Methodology vs Software Dev Framework
rows.summary: Update TAD's README, OBJECTIVES, and docs to reflect the evidence-backed value proposition: TAD is a capability acquisition methodology (human + AI + documents compounding), not a software dev methodology competing with Devin/LangGraph.
rows.cost_rationale: 3 document updates; no code changes required.
rows.value_rationale: The repositioning is evidence-backed by real cross-domain usage; aligns TAD's narrative with its demonstrated value to the only real user.
rows.deliverable: Updated `README.md` with new positioning section + updated `OBJECTIVES.md` with revised goals + a `docs/value-proposition.md` grounded in cross-domain usage evidence from 14 projects.

rows.title: Deprecate Domain Pack YAML — Migrate 9 Remaining YAML Packs to Capability Pack Format
rows.summary: Convert the 9 remaining .tad/domains/*.yaml packs (hw-circuit-design, hw-enclosure, hw-firmware, hw-testing, mobile-development, mobile-release, mobile-testing, mobile-ui-design, supply-chain-security) to SKILL.md + references/ format and retire the dead YAML mechanism.
rows.cost_rationale: 9 pack rebuilds each requiring research + SKILL.md + references/; multi-phase Epic work.
rows.value_rationale: Removes 7,132 lines of dead YAML and dead router hook; Capability Pack format produces higher quality judgment rules per research evidence.
rows.deliverable: 9 new `.claude/skills/{pack-name}.md` files with references/ directories, 9 YAML files deleted from `.tad/domains/`, and a retirement note in CHANGELOG confirming domain-router hook decommissioned.

rows.title: Add rm -rf / DROP TABLE deny patterns to PreToolUse hook
rows.summary: Pending item to wire destructive command deny patterns (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, etc.) into the PreToolUse hook so they are blocked before execution.
rows.cost_rationale: Single file edit to settings.json deny list plus one fixture; no new abstraction needed.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents accidental destructive operations during agent execution — concrete safety improvement with zero ongoing maintenance cost.
rows.deliverable: .claude/settings.json deny-list entries for destructive bash patterns + a test fixture showing the hook blocks rm -rf and DROP TABLE calls with a clear error message.

rows.title: Automated TAD behavior validation agent
rows.summary: IDEA-20260401: Validate that TAD agents actually follow the protocol (Socratic questioning, expert review, gate compliance) via automated behavioral tests rather than manual spot-checks.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow file plus synthetic task design plus protocol trace parser; significant design + implementation work.
rows.value_rationale: The trajectory eval harness proves that gates catch real P0s; a self-test agent would catch protocol drift before it ships — high leverage for framework quality.
rows.deliverable: .claude/workflows/tad-self-test.workflow.js that runs a synthetic TAD task end-to-end and verifies: Gate 1-4 were invoked, Layer 2 expert review ran, completion report was filed — with PASS/FAIL output and diff against expected protocol trace.

rows.title: Close Edit-Time Parity Drift Window — Blake Completion Protocol Parity Check
rows.summary: Add a `release-verify.sh parity` check to Blake's handoff completion protocol so that any edit to .claude/skills/ automatically triggers a parity sync to .agents/skills/ before the session ends, closing the window between edit and *publish.
rows.cost_rationale: Single file update to blake/SKILL.md completion protocol; no new infrastructure needed.
rows.value_rationale: Closes a confirmed drift window that caused Codex to run stale protocols; high confidence because the fix and verification mechanism already exist.
rows.deliverable: Updated `blake/SKILL.md` completion checklist with a mandatory parity check step: 'after any handoff touching .claude/skills/, run release-verify.sh parity and auto-copy drifted files'.

rows.title: HTML-based human judgment interface for non-code artifacts
rows.summary: Proven in Colin voice project (3 working prototypes): AI generates artifact + HTML review interface; human gives structured feedback via UI; JSON flows back to AI. Formalizes the human-AI feedback loop for audio, video, brand, and design artifacts.
rows.cost_rationale: Template HTML + schema integration is 2-4 files; moderate effort to wire into Gate 4 non-code branch.
rows.value_rationale: Proven across 3 prototypes in a live project; addresses the rubber-stamp problem for non-code artifacts by giving humans a structured judgment surface instead of open-ended verification.
rows.deliverable: feedback-ui.html template + JSON schema (feedback-json-schema.md already exists at v1.0) integration guide + TAD Gate 4 hook that reads the JSON output for non-code deliverables.

rows.title: Local Skill Capture — *save-skill Command for In-Project Reusable Patterns
rows.summary: New *save-skill command captures a reusable pattern from conversation context into .claude/skills/local/ using LLM draft + user edit, isolated from *sync overwrite, enabling bottom-up skill creation at the moment of discovery.
rows.cost_rationale: New skill file plus local/ subdirectory convention; 2-3 files.
rows.value_rationale: Enables in-the-moment skill capture that compounds knowledge without waiting for Gate 4 KA cycle.
rows.deliverable: A `.claude/skills/save-skill.md` skill file that creates a structured skill file under `.claude/skills/local/` from conversation context, with frontmatter tagging it as local-only.

rows.title: AI/Human Domain Awareness — Embed Rubber Stamp Defense into Gate 4 Protocol
rows.summary: Operationalize the AI/Human domain awareness principle (already in principles.md) into Gate 4 protocol: gate should only ask humans 'only-human-can-answer' questions (direction, perception, taste) and route technical verification to Gate 3 subagents.
rows.cost_rationale: Modifies gate/SKILL.md plus possible updates to alex/SKILL.md Gate 4 ownership definition; 2 files.
rows.value_rationale: Addresses academically validated Rubber Stamp Effect; principles.md captures the theory but Gate 4 protocol is where the rubber meets the road.
rows.deliverable: Updated `gate/SKILL.md` Gate 4 section with explicit domain-split checklist: AI-domain questions routed to Gate 3 subagent, human-domain questions presented as choice options (not validation prompts), with 3 before/after examples.

rows.title: Saveable Skills from Conversation — One-Click Workflow Capture (Linear Agent Pattern)
rows.summary: Let users save a reusable workflow directly from conversation context with a single *save-workflow command, complementing local-skill-capture with a more discoverable UX inspired by Linear Agent's Skills system.
rows.cost_rationale: New skill file + local/ directory; overlaps with local-skill-capture so coordination needed.
rows.value_rationale: Natural in-conversation skill emergence lowers the barrier to knowledge capture vs the formal Gate 4 KA cycle.
rows.deliverable: A `.claude/skills/save-workflow.md` skill that extracts the current conversation's workflow steps into a structured `.claude/skills/local/{workflow-name}.md` file with auto-detected trigger keywords and usage instructions.

rows.title: TAD universal methodology skeleton for non-dev domains
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Extract TAD methodology as a domain-agnostic process framework usable by non-developers (design, research, writing, audio production), distinct from the code-specific implementation.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires synthesis across multiple domains and careful abstraction to avoid software-specific assumptions; L-level design work.
rows.value_rationale: Enables TAD to expand beyond software development — the user's stated goal (see memory); already validated in practice via voice studio and academic research pack.
rows.deliverable: tad-methodology.md core document (domain-agnostic two-agent / four-gate / knowledge-layer model) + one concrete example instantiation for a non-dev domain (e.g., voice production or academic research).

rows.title: 6-line openai.yaml capability pack Codex adapter
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Add a minimal codex-adapter.yaml per capability pack so Codex CLI can load pack rules natively via its YAML config format, without needing the full SKILL.md synthesis.
rows.cost_rationale: Schema spec + one install.sh extension + one demo pack; S-sized if kept to the 6-line format.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces friction for Codex users to consume capability packs; small concrete artifact with broad downstream impact across all 24 packs.
rows.deliverable: codex-adapter.yaml format spec + install.sh extension that writes it to .agents/skills/{pack}/ + one demo pack (e.g., web-backend) with a working adapter verified by codex exec.

rows.title: Triple-Question KA — Workflow Candidate Detection (Q3) Implementation
rows.summary: IDEA-20260603-triple-question-ka-evolution is 'promoted' but completely absent from the backlog. The current Knowledge Assessment only asks 2 questions (new knowledge? new skill?). The third question — 'new workflow candidate?' — was designed with full trigger-point mapping (7 Alex + Blake trigger points) but never implemented. Without Q3 the workflow improvement loop is broken: orchestration patterns discovered in practice never get captured as .workflow.js candidates.
rows.cost_rationale: 2-3 files modified (both agent SKILLs are SAFETY-adjacent); 1 new template; moderate complexity.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1 lists 'candidate upgrade directions extracted from research' — workflow capture closure is a concrete direction from the 2026-06-03 dynamic-workflow research; the idea was explicitly labeled HIGH priority by the user in the idea file, but it has never moved past 'promoted'.
rows.deliverable: alex/SKILL.md and blake/SKILL.md updated to include Q3 at all 7 identified KA trigger points; /Users/sheldonzhao/01-on progress programs/TAD/.tad/templates/workflow-candidate-template.md (WCAND-*.md format); smoke test showing Q3 prompt fires correctly in a *discuss session exit KA

rows.title: Framework component integrity check (session-health.sh)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260403: A script that verifies core TAD framework components are correctly wired: SKILL.md presence, hook registration in settings.json, version consistency, pack registry coherence.
rows.cost_rationale: Single new script; no new abstraction; straightforward check logic.
rows.value_rationale: Provides fast diagnosis of framework drift that currently requires manual inspection across many files; reusable after every sync.
rows.deliverable: session-health.sh with checks for: SKILL.md presence on both platforms, hook wiring in settings.json, version.txt == tad.sh TARGET_VERSION, pack-registry.yaml count matching installed packs — exit 0 or annotated FAIL report.

rows.title: TAD Unified Auth Layer — tad auth status Command and SessionStart Integration
rows.summary: Build a `tad auth status` command that checks gh, codex, gemini, and NotebookLM authentication in a unified table, integrates into SessionStart hook for proactive expiry warnings, and migrates token storage to macOS Keyring where possible.
rows.cost_rationale: New script plus hook modification; 2-3 files, but requires testing all 4 auth mechanisms.
rows.value_rationale: Auth failures derail sessions repeatedly; proactive warning at session start prevents mid-task interruptions.
rows.deliverable: A `tad-auth-check.sh` script producing a 4-row status table (tool | mechanism | status | expires) plus SessionStart hook integration that warns if any tool auth is expired or expiring within 7 days.

rows.title: Cross-project skill promotion mechanism (T3 harvest)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260407: Mechanism to scan skills across multiple downstream projects and promote patterns that appear in >=2 projects into the TAD framework (T3 in the T1/T2/T3 tier model).
rows.cost_rationale: New script + multi-project scan logic + integration into existing *harvest; 2-3 files modified or created.
rows.value_rationale: Completes the T3 tier of the skill lifecycle system; enables empirically-grounded knowledge promotion rather than single-session discoveries.
rows.deliverable: harvest-cross-project.sh script that scans registered project traces/skills for collision detection and emits a promotion-candidates report (>=2 project threshold) + integration into *harvest command.

rows.title: Restructure Gate 4 to separate AI-domain checks from human-domain questions
rows.summary: IDEA-20260703 + principles.md AI/Human Judgment Domain Awareness: Gate 4 currently asks humans to verify things AI should verify (rubber stamp effect). Restructure so Gate 3 subagent handles all technical/logical verification, Gate 4 only asks humans questions only humans can answer (direction, taste, perception).
rows.cost_rationale: Touches Gate 4 structure in SKILL.md (SAFETY-adjacent); requires careful domain classification per step, needs expert review.
rows.value_rationale: Fixes the rubber stamp problem at the gate level — humans currently confirm things they cannot meaningfully evaluate, destroying the safety net that Gate 4 is supposed to provide.
rows.deliverable: Updated gate/SKILL.md Gate 4 step with explicit human-domain-only question list + AI-domain checklist moved to Gate 3; verified with a Gate 4 example showing zero technical verification questions routed to human.

rows.title: EPIC Goal-Driven Research Phase 3: Research-Decision Loop
rows.summary: Remaining phase of EPIC-20260504: add decision traceability (--caller flag) so research outputs are linked to the decision that triggered them, enabling retrospective analysis of research quality.
rows.cost_rationale: Extends existing Epic (3/4 done); SKILL.md update + output format spec; M-sized.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the research accountability loop — currently research is commissioned but its influence on decisions is untracked, making ROI measurement impossible.
rows.deliverable: --caller flag added to *research command + decision-traceability.md output format where each research finding cites the decision it was commissioned for + one end-to-end trace showing caller→research→decision linkage.

rows.title: MCP + Skill Boundary Audit — Classify 25 Packs for Tool-Wrapping Rules
rows.summary: IDEA-20260623-mcp-skill-complementarity is 'promoted' but absent from the backlog. The YOLO Audit cross-model findings (Codex + Gemini) flagged zero collision detection between packs as a gap. No principled model exists for which rules belong in SKILL.md (judgment) vs which are tool-wrapping candidates for MCP. With 25 packs and 10 workflows, the boundary is increasingly blurry. An audit classifying each pack's tool-wrapping rules would surface the 3 highest-ROI MCP conversion candidates and produce a decision record for whether single-user CLI context justifies MCP investment.
rows.cost_rationale: Read-only analysis of 25 SKILL.md files; single output report; no code changes.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR3 requires capability gaps identified with severity — the judgment-vs-MCP boundary is a concrete architectural gap that the 2026-06-23 AI Tinkerers Maestro research surfaced; the audit either confirms MCP investment is warranted or formally closes the question with evidence.
rows.deliverable: /Users/sheldonzhao/01-on progress programs/TAD/.tad/evidence/research/mcp-skill-boundary-audit-2026-07.md — per-pack classification table (25 packs), top 3 MCP conversion candidates with ROI estimate, and a 1-page decision record (invest / defer / reject) grounded in the single-user CLI L1 principle

rows.title: TAD Universal Methodology Skeleton — Domain-Agnostic Framework Beyond Software Dev
rows.summary: Extract the Alex/Blake dual-agent + 4-Gate + knowledge accumulation skeleton as a domain-agnostic framework, letting Capability Packs carry domain-specific knowledge, enabling TAD to work for content, research, audio, finance, and other non-dev domains.
rows.cost_rationale: Large design effort; requires rethinking multiple skill files and creating new template infrastructure.
rows.value_rationale: TAD Universal Method is the stated strategic direction in MEMORY notes; evidence from 14 cross-domain projects confirms the core loop generalizes.
rows.deliverable: A `docs/tad-universal-methodology.md` framework document + an `alex/SKILL-universal-template.md` that parameterizes away all software-specific assumptions, validated by running it on one non-dev domain (e.g., podcast production).

rows.title: Structured Feedback Collector — HTML Feedback Interface for Human Judgment
rows.summary: AI generates both an artifact (code, page, audio, brand) and a structured HTML feedback form alongside it. Human fills the form, exports JSON, and the AI precisely applies the structured changes — replacing ambiguous natural-language feedback.
rows.cost_rationale: New CLI tool + skill file + HTML template system; large scope but self-contained.
rows.value_rationale: Solves the structured human-AI feedback gap that plagues all non-code artifact workflows; applicable across Voice Studio, design, branding, and publishing.
rows.deliverable: A CLI command (or TAD skill) that takes an artifact path and generates a paired `{artifact}-feedback.html` form with labeled UI elements; plus a feedback-apply script that reads the exported JSON and maps fields to specific edit instructions.

rows.title: Unified agent detection and invocation protocol across platforms
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Cross-agent invocation infrastructure — a unified protocol for detecting which agent runtime is active (Claude Code vs Codex vs others) and routing tool calls accordingly, beyond the current platform-detection in tad.sh.
rows.cost_rationale: Guide doc + SKILL.md update + one verification; M-sized.
rows.value_rationale: The agent-computer-interface pack (born from Claude spending 10+ min failing to find Chrome) shows this is a real pain point; a protocol prevents it for all future cross-platform tasks.
rows.deliverable: agent-adapter.md guide documenting the detection protocol + blake/SKILL.md update with a cross-agent routing table (when to use ToolSearch, when to use direct MCP, when to fall back to grep) + one verified cross-agent handoff using the protocol.

rows.title: EPIC Agent Capability Packs — remaining phases (real validation, cross-agent, template)
rows.summary: EPIC-20260507 is 6/9 phases done. Remaining: Phase 2 (real project validation — use packs in menu-snap, measure quality delta), Phase 3 (cross-agent validation — same pack on Codex), Phase 4 (template extraction — CONSUMES/PRODUCES standard).
rows.cost_rationale: Three Epic phases each requiring real-project task execution, cross-platform validation, and template authoring for 24 packs.
rows.value_rationale: Completing pack validation closes the 'validation theater' gap identified in the YOLO audit; real project deltas are the only proof packs improve agent behavior.
rows.deliverable: Pack validation report for menu-snap (quality-delta before/after for >=2 packs) + Codex cross-agent validation result + CONSUMES/PRODUCES template applied to all 24 packs.

rows.title: Fix classify_scope: bracket-class word-boundary for hook/trace/registry globs
rows.summary: Unbounded substring globs *hook*/*trace*/*registry* false-classify project slugs (webhook-handler→framework, registry-of-products→framework), causing framework candidates to incorrectly fan out cross-project in *evolve.
rows.cost_rationale: One hook file, pattern replacement; S-sized.
rows.value_rationale: False-positive framework classification causes cross-project evolution noise; fix is known and low-risk.
rows.deliverable: Updated classify_scope with bracket-class word-boundary patterns (per architecture.md 2026-04-24) + fixture showing webhook-handler classified as project not framework.

rows.title: Unify tad.sh version stamp: 2-part TARGET_VERSION vs 3-part source
rows.summary: tad.sh stamps downstream version.txt = 2-part MAJOR.MINOR via TARGET_VERSION:537, while source version.txt is 3-part MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH. Deferred from release cycle; decide unified scheme and fix.
rows.cost_rationale: Two-file change with a clear decision to make; S-sized.
rows.value_rationale: Version inconsistency between source and downstream causes confusing version-gate noise; small fix with permanent clarity benefit.
rows.deliverable: Updated tad.sh using 3-part version throughout (or documented decision for 2-part with CHANGELOG entry) + release-verify.sh version check updated to match + all downstream projects stamped consistently.

rows.title: Cross-Project Skill Harvest — *skill-harvest Command for Promoting Local Skills
rows.summary: New *skill-harvest command scans sync-registry.yaml projects for local skills, generates a cross-project index, and lets users promote/copy/archive skills with Alex polishing candidates to framework quality before *sync distribution.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow + script + skill file; depends on local-skill-capture landing first.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the upward knowledge flow gap (local → framework) that makes the two-way skill economy possible.
rows.deliverable: A `.claude/skills/skill-harvest.md` skill file + `harvest-scanner.sh` script that produces a cross-project skill index table and accepts promote/copy/archive decisions.

rows.title: Domain Pack Monthly Refresh — Tools and Skills Periodic Update Mechanism
rows.summary: Create a repeatable monthly handoff template that audits tools-registry.yaml for stale versions, scans for new MCP servers/CLI tools, and updates domain.yaml skills research.
rows.cost_rationale: Single template file, no tooling changes required.
rows.value_rationale: Keeps packs current with fast-moving tool ecosystem; low cost, repeatable value.
rows.deliverable: A `.tad/templates/domain-pack-refresh-handoff.md` template that Blake can execute monthly, with explicit checklist and evidence trail format.

rows.title: Capability Pack Codex YAML Adapter — Auto-generate openai.yaml from SKILL.md
rows.summary: Add a --agent=codex flag to each capability pack install.sh that auto-generates a minimal openai.yaml adapter file, giving TAD packs zero-maintenance Codex compatibility without a separate AGENTS.md routing table.
rows.cost_rationale: Single script update plus 1-2 generated YAML files per pack; contained scope.
rows.value_rationale: Zero-cost Codex parity is a recurring need; automating adapter generation removes a manual step from every pack release.
rows.deliverable: Updated `install.sh` with --agent=codex flag that generates `.agents/skills/{pack-name}-openai.yaml` with the 5 fields Codex reads (display_name, short_description, brand_color, default_prompt, allow_implicit_invocation).

rows.title: Deterministic Rules Over LLM for Classification (Daria's Desk Pattern)
rows.summary: Audit TAD's current LLM-based intent routing and identify which classification decisions have clear enough patterns (greeting vs command, *publish vs *sync) to be replaced with 200-line deterministic rules, reducing cost and latency.
rows.cost_rationale: Audit document plus one small script change; contained and reversible.
rows.value_rationale: Faster and cheaper routing for predictable command patterns; Daria's Desk validates the pattern for bounded classification tasks.
rows.deliverable: A classification audit report at `.tad/evidence/designs/deterministic-vs-llm-audit.md` listing each TAD routing decision with a deterministic-feasibility score, plus one concrete implementation replacing the lowest-complexity LLM routing with a regex/heuristic equivalent.

rows.title: Production Observation Practice — TAD Deployed Agent Trace Review Protocol
rows.summary: Add a post-deployment observation module to TAD's lifecycle: a lightweight 'Traces Review' practice (inspired by PostHog's weekly Traces Hour) that lets agent designers analyze real production traces to find failure categories that automated evals miss.
rows.cost_rationale: Single pattern document; no tooling changes in initial version.
rows.value_rationale: Fills the confirmed gap in TAD's lifecycle after Gate 4; closes the design→deploy→observe→improve loop that currently ends at delivery.
rows.deliverable: A new `.tad/project-knowledge/production-observation.md` pattern file documenting the TAD post-deployment loop: what trace data to collect, how often to review, how to feed findings back into Gate 3 / principles.md.

rows.title: Session Health Check — Framework Component Integrity Verification at Startup
rows.summary: Extend SessionStart hook to verify hook script existence, config version consistency, and template file completeness — catching installation/sync issues before any agent work begins.
rows.cost_rationale: Modifies session-start.sh plus adds a health-check lib file; 2-3 files total.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents silent broken installations from causing mysterious mid-session failures; complements release-verify.sh structural checks.
rows.deliverable: Extended `.tad/hooks/session-start.sh` with integrity checks producing a structured health report; any critical failure outputs WARNING to the session start banner.

rows.title: TAD as Opt-In Mode — Flip CLAUDE.md from Ambient Law to Deliberate Entry
rows.summary: Change CLAUDE.md so TAD strict discipline only activates on explicit /alex /blake /gate invocations. Free-form chat does not trigger routing overhead. Capability packs still auto-load on domain keywords.
rows.cost_rationale: CLAUDE.md plus alex skill trigger update; 2 files but the changes are architecturally significant.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces false-positive friction and aligns with user's 'just do it' feedback pattern; makes TAD more accessible without reducing quality when explicitly engaged.
rows.deliverable: Updated `CLAUDE.md` routing section replacing probabilistic auto-routing with explicit-invocation model + updated alex/SKILL.md with clarified trigger conditions that no longer fire on ambient chat.

rows.title: Self-Critique Generation Pipeline — Generate, Self-Evaluate, Filter
rows.summary: Apply the 'generate → self-critique → filter → keep only passing outputs' pattern to TAD research findings and handoff documents: generated content passes through a self-evaluation step before being accepted, reducing low-quality outputs without human review.
rows.cost_rationale: Modifies research flow + alex skill; 2-3 files, builds on existing *research infrastructure.
rows.value_rationale: Self-critique is the externalized version of expert review; applying it to generated research findings and handoffs improves quality without adding human review cost.
rows.deliverable: Updated `*research` flow with a self-critique verification step that evaluates each finding against a 5-point rubric before adding to the NotebookLM corpus, plus a handoff quality self-check in alex/SKILL.md.

rows.title: Bundle: Dream scanner Pass C — dedup, scope mis-tag, fromjson error guard
rows.summary: Three pending bugs in dream-scanner Pass C: (a) no dedup against existing project-knowledge before emit; (b) file=null on decision_point causes framework overrides to mis-classify as project scope; (c) fromjson error on malformed context yields junk candidate instead of safe fallback.
rows.cost_rationale: Three targeted fixes in one hook file; no new abstraction; S-sized bundle.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents junk/duplicate candidates from polluting the dream queue, improving the signal-to-noise ratio of the auto-evolve pipeline.
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner hook with all three fixes + 3 fixtures: (a) duplicate suppressed, (b) file=null classified as framework, (c) malformed context → 'unknown' with no junk candidate emitted.

rows.title: Fix emit_decision_points: re-bind havehdr on new Decision Summary tables
rows.summary: emit_decision_points locks havehdr on the FIRST Decision Summary table and never re-binds, so 2nd+ decision tables in one §11 are silently dropped. Also a trailing non-Decision table over-emits with stale indices.
rows.cost_rationale: Single hook file fix with clear logic change; S-sized.
rows.value_rationale: Silent data loss in multi-table handoffs corrupts the trace record used by *optimize and trajectory eval; worth fixing before more traces accumulate.
rows.deliverable: Updated hook with havehdr re-bind logic when a fresh row's cells are decision+chosen + fixture proving a 2-table §11 emits both tables' decisions correctly.

rows.title: Add express: true frontmatter field consumed by layer2-audit
rows.summary: layer2-audit currently uses slug-naming convention as proxy for express tier, causing false >=2-reviewer WARN for any express handoff that forgets 'express' in its slug. Durable fix: express: true frontmatter field that layer2-audit reads directly.
rows.cost_rationale: 2-file change (template + audit script); clear spec already defined by backend-architect review.
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates recurring false-positive review warnings that erode trust in layer2-audit output.
rows.deliverable: Handoff template updated with optional express: true field + layer2-audit consumer that reads the field + test fixture showing no false WARN on an express handoff without 'express' in slug.

rows.title: Run *optimize on menu-snap to surface execution patterns from 14 traces
rows.summary: Pending operational task: run *optimize on the menu-snap project which has 14 trace files, to surface recurring patterns and improvement candidates for the first time since trajectory eval harness landed.
rows.cost_rationale: Running an existing command and producing a report; S-sized operational task.
rows.value_rationale: First real *optimize run after auto-evolve epic and trajectory eval; will validate the pipeline with real data and produce actionable signal.
rows.deliverable: *optimize output report for menu-snap (.tad/evidence/optimize-menu-snap-YYYY-MM-DD.md) listing the top 3 actionable improvement candidates with supporting trace citations.

rows.title: TAD Self-Test Agent — Automated Agent Behavior Verification
rows.summary: Create a sub-agent-based automated test harness that verifies TAD hook triggers, skill loading, config integrity, and SessionStart behavior — replacing the current manual open-terminal → run flow → observe cycle.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires new test scripts + runner infrastructure touching 2-4 files.
rows.value_rationale: Enables regression safety after upgrades, but open questions about sub-agent nesting remain.
rows.deliverable: A `.tad/tests/` directory with a runner script and at least 3 PASS/FAIL checks for hooks, skills, and config completeness, returning a structured test report.

rows.title: TAD_* environment variable config override for tad.sh settings
rows.summary: IDEA-20260403: Allow TAD settings to be overridden via TAD_* environment variables (e.g. TAD_RELEASE_GATE=warn, TAD_SYNC_DRY_RUN=1) without editing files, enabling non-interactive CI use.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-file env-var section + documentation; S-sized.
rows.value_rationale: Enables non-interactive CI usage without file edits; already partially needed (TAD_RELEASE_GATE=warn exists for shadow mode).
rows.deliverable: tad.sh env-var parsing section + documented override table in README (variable name, default, effect) + CI example in docs/.

rows.title: Design and implement title/discovery-match semantic dedup for dream candidates
rows.summary: Current grep-on-.decision/.chosen dedup is inert (0/31 real match). Need title/discovery match or embedding-based semantic dedup to prevent the same idea from entering the queue repeatedly.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires designing a similarity metric that works in bash/jq; moderate complexity for 2 files.
rows.value_rationale: Without dedup, the dream queue accumulates the same ideas across sessions, diluting signal for *evolve and *surplus.
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner with title-similarity dedup (normalized string match or jq-based token overlap) + fixture showing a near-duplicate candidate is suppressed on second emit.

rows.title: Dream scanner auto-scope via git remote hash + 2-project promotion threshold
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: Dream scanner currently has manual scope; add auto-scope using git remote hash to detect which project the trace belongs to, and enforce the 2-project promotion threshold before elevating a pattern to framework-level.
rows.cost_rationale: Git integration + threshold logic; 2-file change; M-sized.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents premature promotion of single-project patterns to the framework knowledge base, improving knowledge quality.
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner with git-remote-hash-based project detection + 2-project threshold check against existing project-knowledge + fixture showing a single-project pattern is held vs a 2-project pattern promoted.

rows.title: Unified Agent Adapter Pattern — TAD on Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor
rows.summary: Build a unified agent detection and invocation protocol layer (tad-adapter.sh) that auto-detects Claude Code vs Codex vs Gemini CLI runtime and routes SKILL.md loading appropriately, enabling TAD Universal Method direction.
rows.cost_rationale: New script infrastructure plus agent-specific adapters; large unknowns around multi-turn protocol differences.
rows.value_rationale: Supports the TAD Universal Method direction but depends on unresolved protocol differences between single-round and multi-round agent invocations.
rows.deliverable: A `.tad/hooks/lib/tad-adapter.sh` script that detects the active agent runtime and emits the correct SKILL.md invocation for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI, with integration tests for all 3 platforms.

rows.title: Formalize weekly trace review as TAD workflow
rows.summary: IDEA-20260703 from PostHog: weekly team trace review sessions produce more value than building eval datasets. Formalize this as a TAD workflow with a review rubric and structured output.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow file + rubric document; M-sized design + implementation.
rows.value_rationale: Low-infra practice that has proven ROI in the field; formalizing it makes it repeatable and scalable across the 14 downstream projects.
rows.deliverable: .claude/workflows/trace-review.workflow.js + trace-review-rubric.md (5 dimensions, PASS/FAIL per trace, actionable improvement list) + one pilot run producing a real trace-review report.

rows.title: Dream Scanner Auto-Scope Detection via Git Remote Hash
rows.summary: Extend dream-scanner.sh to auto-detect project vs framework scope by hashing the git remote URL, and auto-suggest scope_tag: framework when the same candidate pattern appears across 2+ registered projects in sync-registry.yaml.
rows.cost_rationale: Modification to a single existing script.
rows.value_rationale: *dream loop itself has near-zero historical yield; scope detection reduces friction but does not fix the low yield problem.
rows.deliverable: Updated `.tad/hooks/lib/dream-scanner.sh` that appends a `detected_scope` field to each candidate based on git-remote hash cross-referencing, with a 2-project threshold test.

rows.title: Single-Loop Agent No-Framework Architecture Validation (PostHog Pattern)
rows.summary: Document TAD's alignment with PostHog's no-framework single-loop agent architecture and evaluate if TAD's multi-skill orchestration could benefit from a dual-model strategy (Claude Sonnet for main loop, cost-effective model for specific reasoning steps).
rows.cost_rationale: Design document only; no code changes unless a specific optimization is accepted.
rows.value_rationale: Confirms existing architecture rather than changing it; incremental value from dual-model strategy.
rows.deliverable: An ADR at `.tad/evidence/designs/single-loop-validation-adr.md` confirming TAD's architecture matches single-loop principles and recommending specific TAD steps (if any) that would benefit from a cheaper reasoning model.

rows.title: Fix stale carry-forward doc mentions from Self-Evolution Pruning
rows.summary: Stale T2-era mentions in intent-router-protocol.md L150/L198 + accept-command.md L251 + handoff-a-to-b.md L24 left over from the Self-Evolution Pruning Epic cleanup pass.
rows.cost_rationale: 4 targeted line edits across 3 files; trivial S-sized cleanup.
rows.value_rationale: Cosmetic but prevents future confusion when maintainers read protocol docs that reference retired concepts.
rows.deliverable: 3 files updated with stale references removed or corrected — git diff showing only the 4 targeted lines changed, verified by grep confirming no remaining stale T2 terminology.

rows.title: Cheap Deterministic Loop + Expensive LLM Hybrid Architecture (petri Pattern)
rows.summary: Apply petri's 'cheap deterministic inner loop + expensive LLM as variation operator' pattern to TAD: identify which TAD components can run deterministically (e.g., document health check, version gate) and only invoke LLM at key judgment nodes.
rows.cost_rationale: ADR document plus one concrete implementation change; contained scope.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces LLM token cost for mechanical checks; pattern validation needed before broader adoption.
rows.deliverable: An ADR at `.tad/evidence/designs/cheap-loop-expensive-llm-adr.md` mapping each current TAD loop (health check, version gate, dream scanner) to either 'deterministic' or 'LLM-required', plus one concrete refactor converting document health check to deterministic mode.

rows.title: State Machine Multi-Stream Sync for Agent Interactions (Simer Pattern)
rows.summary: Apply Simer's phase-based state machine + multi-stream context synchronization to TAD's Alex/Blake protocol: formalize phase entry/exit conditions and ensure all relevant data streams (code state, conversation history, handoff) are synchronized at phase transitions.
rows.cost_rationale: Design document only; TAD already has structured phases so this is formalization.
rows.value_rationale: TAD already implements phased protocol; formal state machine notation adds rigor but limited practical improvement for a single-user CLI.
rows.deliverable: An ADR at `.tad/evidence/designs/state-machine-protocol-adr.md` mapping the existing Alex/Blake phases to a formal state machine with entry/exit conditions, identifying any phase transitions that currently lack explicit synchronization.

rows.title: TAD Config Environment Variable Override Layer
rows.summary: Add a 4-layer config priority (default → file → env → CLI) inspired by OpenHarness, enabling TAD_MODEL and TAD_CONFIG_PATH env vars to override YAML file settings.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires modifying config loading across 2-3 hook library files.
rows.value_rationale: TAD is a single-user CLI with no CI/CD deployment scenario confirmed; env override adds complexity for minimal current benefit.
rows.deliverable: Updated config loading logic in `.tad/hooks/lib/` that reads TAD_* env vars with documented naming convention, plus 2 integration tests proving override precedence.

rows.title: Hook Timeout Configuration — Prevent Hung Hook Scripts
rows.summary: Add timeout_seconds to each hook type definition in settings.json to prevent TAD from waiting indefinitely when a hook script hangs, matching OpenHarness patterns (30s command/http, 60s agent).
rows.cost_rationale: Single file change plus documentation; lowest cost tier.
rows.value_rationale: Unclear if Claude Code already enforces hook timeouts natively; must verify before implementing to avoid redundant work.
rows.deliverable: Updated `.claude/settings.json` hook definitions with timeout_seconds fields and a one-page doc explaining the timeout behavior and defaults.

rows.title: DeerFlow Pattern Borrowing — Sandbox, Persistent Memory, Message Gateway
rows.summary: Study DeerFlow 2.0 (ByteDance, 45K stars) and adopt its container sandbox isolation, two-layer session+profile memory, and multi-channel message gateway for non-dev TAD users.
rows.cost_rationale: Single design document, no code changes needed at this stage.
rows.value_rationale: TAD already exceeds DeerFlow on quality gates and human-bridge; borrowing is incremental and speculative for a single-user CLI.
rows.deliverable: An ADR file `.tad/evidence/designs/deerflow-patterns-adr.md` documenting which of the 3 patterns TAD should adopt, with rationale and implementation scope for each.

rows.title: CRDT + Agent Safe-Boundary Hot-Swap for Real-Time Collaboration (Jam Pattern)
rows.summary: Apply Jam's Yjs CRDT + bar-boundary hot-swap architecture to agent collaboration: agents only inject changes at defined safe checkpoints, preventing mid-execution conflicts in shared state.
rows.cost_rationale: Design document only; TAD is currently single-user so implementation would come later.
rows.value_rationale: TAD is a single-user CLI with terminal-isolated agents; CRDT real-time collaboration is premature but the safe-boundary principle is worth capturing.
rows.deliverable: An ADR at `.tad/evidence/designs/crdt-agent-collab-adr.md` evaluating whether CRDT-based shared state applies to TAD's Alex/Blake handoff protocol, with a concrete proposal or explicit rejection with rationale.

rows.title: JAX vmap Extreme Parallelism for Agent Pack Evaluation
rows.summary: Apply JAX's vmap/pmap pattern (Jaxpot: 100M steps/sec on single GPU) to run thousands of agent prompt variants simultaneously for capability pack behavioral evaluation.
rows.cost_rationale: Spike + prototype; requires GPU access and JAX setup.
rows.value_rationale: Interesting for large-scale pack evaluation but requires GPU infrastructure; TAD is a single-user CLI where 10-variant eval suffices.
rows.deliverable: A spike report at `.tad/evidence/research/jax-parallel-eval-spike.md` with a working prototype that runs at least 10 prompt variants in parallel for one capability pack scenario and measures latency vs sequential LLM calls.

rows.title: WebRTC as Agent + ML Model Lightweight Transport Layer
rows.summary: Evaluate WebRTC (via LiveKit) as a low-latency, browser-native transport for TAD-designed agents that need to process real-time video/audio streams alongside ML model inference (e.g., Voice Studio, computer-use agents).
rows.cost_rationale: Spike + prototype; requires LiveKit server setup and Kotlin or Python client.
rows.value_rationale: Niche transport optimization; speculative fit for TAD's current agent portfolio without confirmed latency-sensitive use cases.
rows.deliverable: A spike report at `.tad/evidence/research/webrtc-agent-transport-spike.md` with a minimal LiveKit + agent prototype handling one video stream and one ML model inference, measuring latency and setup complexity.

rows.title: GitAgent Protocol Export — TAD as GAP-Compatible Repo for Cross-Platform Portability
rows.summary: Map TAD's .tad/ directory structure to GitAgent Protocol's agent.yaml + memory/ + skills/ + hooks/ format, enabling TAD skills and knowledge to run on Cursor, OpenClaw, Codex, and Gemini CLI without per-platform adaptation.
rows.cost_rationale: Protocol compatibility mapping requires new directory structure plus adapter scripts; unknown compatibility risks.
rows.value_rationale: GAP protocol maturity and adoption are unvalidated; multi-platform portability is directionally correct but the specific protocol may not be the right bet.
rows.deliverable: A `tad-gap/` directory with a valid agent.yaml manifest + adapter scripts for GAP's memory/ and skills/ conventions, with one end-to-end test loading TAD skills in a non-Claude GAP-compatible runtime.

rows.title: Self-Evolving System — Domain Pack Auto-Optimization from Execution History
rows.summary: Three-stage pipeline: (1) JSONL trace recording per Domain Pack execution, (2) failure-pattern aggregation into quality_criteria proposals, (3) Meta-Harness-style step redesign with Gate-as-evaluator.
rows.cost_rationale: Large Epic-level effort; Phase 1 alone touches hook infrastructure and 3+ files.
rows.value_rationale: Self-evolution loop yield was near-zero in past experience (dream/evolve/optimize retired); auto-optimization has not proven itself in this codebase.
rows.deliverable: Phase 1 only: a `.tad/evidence/domain-traces/{domain}/{date}.jsonl` schema spec + trace-emitting hook integration producing real trace files for 2 pack runs.

rows.title: Claude Code Automated Reverse Engineering + Game Data Training Pipeline
rows.summary: Investigate using Claude Code to automate reverse engineering workflows (memory-address hunting, binary hooking) as inspired by Originlab's $8M-funded game capture pipeline for world-model training data generation.
rows.cost_rationale: Large exploratory effort requiring specialized tooling and binary analysis expertise.
rows.value_rationale: Interesting capability frontier but orthogonal to TAD's core methodology; no clear near-term application to the project.
rows.deliverable: A POC script that uses Claude Code to locate and extract at least one structured data stream from an open-source game binary, with a written findings report on feasibility and limitations.

rows.title: Release Full Version Sweep Gate — Detect All Stale Version Strings
rows.summary: Add a version-sweep mode to release-verify.sh that regex-scans all git-tracked files for any version string not equal to $NEW (excluding CHANGELOG history and archive dirs), catching files frozen multiple releases back.
rows.cost_rationale: If already implemented (per git log cf88b7f Gate 4 PASS), cost is just status verification.
rows.value_rationale: Already shipped; included only because idea file still shows 'captured' status — should be archived.
rows.deliverable: Note: commit d436498 already added release-verify.sh version-sweep mode. Confirm the implementation covers all cases from this idea file and update the idea status to completed.

rows.title: Imitation Learning + Classical AI Domain Bridge Pattern
rows.summary: Conceptual insight from Tilmann Böhme's Connect Four robot: classical AI handles planning/search, imitation learning handles perception/execution. Evaluate where TAD's agent stack could apply this 'each AI does what it's best at' split.
rows.cost_rationale: Single pattern document; minimal effort.
rows.value_rationale: Primarily a conceptual refinement of the 'cheap-loop-expensive-llm' idea with no additional concrete next step for TAD.
rows.deliverable: A short entry in `.tad/project-knowledge/patterns/ai-domain-bridge.md` documenting the pattern with 2 concrete TAD application examples (e.g., version gate = classical; handoff quality = LLM).

rows.title: Domain Pack Taxonomy Reorganization — Vertical vs Cross-Cutting Two-Axis Structure
rows.summary: Reorganize 21 Domain Packs from flat vertical grouping into a vertical × cross-cutting two-axis taxonomy to improve discoverability. Explicitly demoted from Epic due to over-engineering with no confirmed user pain.
rows.cost_rationale: Even the minimal tagging approach touches 21 YAML files + registry + README.
rows.value_rationale: Explicitly parked by user as over-engineering with no confirmed discoverability pain; low value until evidence accumulates.
rows.deliverable: Updated keywords.yaml with cross_cutting: true tags + README cross-cutting index section; full rename deferred until ≥2 concrete 'cannot find this pack' incidents documented.
